en: To give up or relinquish control of, to surrender or to give oneself over, or to yield to one's emotions.
example: Many baby girls have been abandoned on the streets of Beijing.
exampleCn: 暴风雨中他们被迫抛弃了那艘船。

en: Suitableness.
example: This phone has the ability to have its software upgraded wirelessly.
exampleCn: 她有能力独立完成这个项目。

en: A state of being away or withdrawn from a place or from companionship; the period of being away.
example: Absence makes the heart grow fonder.
exampleCn: 他的缺席让会议难以进行。

en: That which is independent of context-dependent interpretation, inviolate, fundamental.
example: moral absolutes
exampleCn: 她说的话有绝对的把握。

en: To receive, especially with a consent, with favour, or with approval.
example: The Boy Scouts were going to accept him as a member.
exampleCn: 他欣然接受了这份工作。

en: A way or means of approaching or entering; an entrance; a passage.
example: an access of territory
exampleCn: 学生可以自由访问图书馆。

en: An unexpected event with negative consequences occurring without the intention of the one suffering the consequences.
example: to die by an accident
exampleCn: 一场小车祸堵住了道路。

en: To go with or attend as a companion or associate; to keep company with; to go along with.
example: Geoffrey accompanied the group on their pilgrimage.

en: To finish successfully.
example: to accomplish a design, an object, a promise
exampleCn: 我们终于完成了既定目标。

en: Telling the truth or giving a true result; exact; not defective or faulty
example: accurate knowledge

en: To succeed in something, now especially in academic performance.
example: Show all the spoils by valiant kings achieved.

en: To get.
example: He acquired a title.
exampleCn: 他用一年时间学会了法语。

en: To equalize; to make adequate.
example: an adequate definition

en: To modify.
example: Morimoto's recipes are adjusted to suit the American palate.
exampleCn: 他需要时间适应新环境。

en: To be amazed at; to view with surprise; to marvel at.
example: He had always admired the work ethos and family values of his friend.

en: To allow to enter; to grant entrance (to), whether into a place, into the mind, or into consideration
example: A ticket admits one into a playhouse.

en: To take by choice into relationship (a child, heir, friend, citizen, etc.)
example: He adopted a new look in order to fit in with his new workmates.

en: A forward move; improvement or progression.
example: an advance in health or knowledge

en: Any condition, circumstance, opportunity or means, particularly favorable to success, or to any desired end.
example: The enemy had the advantage of a more elevated position.
exampleCn: 早起让你一天占得先机。

en: The encountering of risks; a bold undertaking, in which dangers are likely to be encountered, and the issue is staked upon unforeseen events; a daring feat.
example: A life full of adventures.

en: An opinion offered in an effort to be helpful.
example: She was offered various piece of advice on what to do with her new-found wealth.

en: To influence or alter.
example: The experience affected me deeply.

en: To incur, stand, or bear without serious detriment, as an act which might under other circumstances be injurious;—with an auxiliary, as can, could, might, etc.; to be able or rich enough.
example: I think we can afford the extra hour it will take.  We can only afford to buy a small car at the moment.

en: (usually used predicatively, not attributively, be afraid) Impressed with fear or apprehension; in fear.
example: He is afraid of death.

en: The capacity, condition, or state of acting or of exerting power.
example: individual agency
exampleCn: 他在一家新闻机构工作。

en: The pointing of a weapon, as a gun, a dart, or an arrow, or object, in the line of direction with the object intended to be struck; the line of fire; the direction of anything, such as a spear, a blow, a discourse, a remark, towards a particular point or object, with a view to strike or affect it.
example: Take time with the aim of your gun.

en: A summons to arms, as on the approach of an enemy.
example: The clock radio is a friendlier version of the cold alarm by the bedside.

en: A book specially designed to keep photographs, stamps, or autographs.
exampleCn: 这是她最新的音乐专辑。

en: Any of a class of organic compounds (such as ethanol) containing a hydroxyl functional group (-OH).
One from whom a person is descended, whether on the father's or mother's side, at any distance of time; a progenitor; a forefather.
example: This fossil animal is regarded as the ancestor of the horse.

en: A feeling of discomfort or vexation caused by what one dislikes.
example: Marc loved his sister, but when she annoyed him he wanted to switch her off.
exampleCn: 蚊子的嗡嗡声让人烦躁。

en: An annual publication; a book, periodical, journal, report, comic book, yearbook, etc., which is published serially once a year, which may or may not be in addition to regular weekly or monthly publication.
example: I read the magazine, but I usually don't purchase the annuals.

en: An unpleasant state of mental uneasiness, nervousness, apprehension and obsession or concern about some uncertain event.
Capable of being seen, or easily seen; open to view; visible to the eye, eyely; within sight or view.
To be grateful or thankful for.
example: I appreciate your efforts

en: To sanction officially; to ratify; to confirm; to set as satisfactory.
example: Although we may disagree with it, we must nevertheless approve the sentence handed down by the court-martial.

en: To come up from a lower to a higher position.
example: to arise from a kneeling posture

en: Any specific feature, part, or element of something.
example: Japan's aging population is an important aspect of its economy.

en: A helpful action or an act of giving.
example: The foundation gave a much needed assist to the shelter.

en: To authenticate by means of belief; to surmise; to suppose to be true, especially without proof
example: We assume that, as her parents were dentists, she knows quite a bit about dentistry.

en: To fasten, to join to (literally and figuratively).
example: An officer is attached to a certain regiment, company, or ship.

en: An attempt to cause damage, injury to, or death of opponent or enemy.
example: They claimed the censorship of the article was an attack on free speech.

en: To listen to (something or someone); to pay attention to; regard; heed.
example: Secretaries attend to correspondence.

en: To pull toward without touching.
example: A magnet attracts iron filings.
exampleCn: 鲜艳的色彩吸引了蜜蜂。

en: A group of people within hearing; specifically, a large gathering of people listening to or watching a performance, speech, etc.
example: We joined the audience just as the lights went down.

en: The originator or creator of a work, especially of a literary composition.
example: Have you read any Corinthian authors?

en: A car with automatic transmission.
example: I never learned to drive a stick. I can only drive an automatic.

en: Such as one may avail oneself of; capable of being used for the accomplishment of a purpose.
example: We have an available candidate.

en: The arithmetic mean.
example: The average of 10, 20 and 24 is (10 + 20 + 24)/3 = 18.
exampleCn: 他的成绩处于中等水平。

en: To try not to meet or communicate with (a person); to shun
example: I try to avoid the company of gamblers.

en: A judgment, sentence, or final decision. Specifically: The decision of arbitrators in a case submitted.
example: the arbitrators awarded damages to the complainant

en: Vigilant or on one's guard against danger or difficulty.
example: Stay aware! Don't let your guard down.

en: Someone or something that is awkward.
example: John was awkward at performing the trick. He'll have to practice to improve.

en: A state in which opposing forces harmonise; equilibrium.
example: Blair thought he could provide a useful balance to Bush's policies.

en: (‘the bare’) The surface, the (bare) skin.
example: a bare majority

en: A structure that bars passage.
A physical base or foundation.
example: Cars must be checked on a yearly basis.

en: To conduct (oneself) well, or in a given way.
example: You need to behave yourself, young lady.
exampleCn: 孩子们在公共场所很乖。

en: Mental acceptance of a claim as true.
example: It's my belief that the thief is somebody known to us.

en: To have its proper place.
example: Where does this document belong?

en: Below or underneath.
An advantage; help or aid from something.
example: It was for her benefit.   His benefit was free beer.

en: The unknown.
example: 2017 July 13, Joseph Gamp "Marvel release stirring new set images of Black Panther and we are BEYOND excited" Metro (UK)
exampleCn: 这超出了我的能力范围。

en: Censure.
example: Blame came from all directions.

en: A push from behind, as to one who is endeavoring to climb.
example: The controversy gave a boost to the author's sales.

en: Fuss, ado.
example: There was a bit of bother at the hairdresser's when they couldn't find my appointment in the book.

en: The woody part of a tree arising from the trunk and usually dividing.
example: the branch of an antler, a chandelier, or a railway

en: A writ summoning one to answer to any action.
example: We got a news brief.

en: A shallow lake, one of a number of bodies of water in eastern Norfolk and Suffolk.
example: the broad expanse of ocean

en: The amount of money or resources earmarked for a particular institution, activity or time-frame.
example: Budgeting is even harder in times of recession

en: A heavy load.
example: a ship of a hundred tons burden
exampleCn: 照顾老人是种负担也是责任。

en: Occurring in abrupt bursts, especially of information
example: The router needs to handle bursty traffic.
exampleCn: 网络流量常有突发特征。

en: A burrow.
example: bury a bone;  bury the embers

en: To determine the value of something or the solution to something by a mathematical process.
example: Calculate the square root of 3 to 10 decimal places.

en: (in a person) The state of being calm; peacefulness; absence of worry, anger, fear or other strong negative emotion.
example: to calm a crying baby
exampleCn: 深吸一口气让自己平静。

en: A series of operations undertaken to achieve a set goal.
example: The company is targeting children in their latest advertising campaign.
exampleCn: 环保宣传活动正在进行。

en: A cancellation (US); (nonstandard in some kinds of English).
example: He cancelled his order on their website.

en: A person who is running in an election.
exampleCn: 她是主席职位的候选人。

en: An act of capturing; a seizing by force or stratagem.
example: the capture of a lover's heart
exampleCn: 相机捕捉到了精彩瞬间。

en: The chemical element (symbol C) with an atomic number of 6. It can be found in pure form for example as graphite, a black, shiny and very soft material, or diamond, a colourless, transparent, crystalline solid and the hardest known material.
example: A methane molecule is made up of a single carbon with four hydrogens.

en: One's calling in life; a person's occupation; one's profession.
example: Washington's career as a soldier

en: A worker who is only working for a company occasionally, not as its permanent employee.
example: The devs dumbed the game down so the casuals could enjoy it.

en: To extol or honour in a solemn manner.
example: to celebrate the name of the Most High

en: A confrontation; a dare.
example: We challenged the boys next door to a game of football.

en: The physical confine of a river or slough, consisting of a bed and banks.
example: The water coming out of the waterwheel created a standing wave in the channel.

en: (authorship) One of the main sections into which the text of a book is divided.
example: Detective novel writers try to keep up the suspense until the last chapter.

en: A being involved in the action of a story.
example: A single locus governing the petal colour character was detected on the linkage group A2.

en: The amount of money levied for a service.
example: There will be a charge of five dollars.

en: An organization, the objective of which is to carry out a charitable purpose.
exampleCn: 她把钱捐给了慈善机构。

en: The act of one who chases another; a pursuit.
example: I need something to chase this shot with.

en: To violate rules in order to gain advantage from a situation.
example: My brother flunked biology because he cheated on his mid-term.

en: A leader or head of a group of people, organisation, etc.
example: All firefighters report to the fire chief.

en: The state of being a child.
An option; a decision; an opportunity to choose or select something.
example: Do I have a choice of what color to paint it?

en: To pick; to make the choice of; to select.
example: I chose a nice ripe apple from the fruit bowl.

en: A resident of a city or town, especially one with legally-recognized rights or duties.
example: Diogenes reckoned himself a citizen of the world.

en: Having to do with people and government office as opposed to the military or religion.
example: She went into civil service because she wanted to help the people.

en: A demand of ownership made for something.
example: a claim of ownership

en: (of liquids, such as wine or syrup) To make clear or bright by freeing from feculent matter
example: Leave the wine for 24 hours and it will clarify.

en: Fully sufficient; found in copious supply; in great quantity; overflowing.
example: [W]ith their magical words they [poets] bring forth to our eyesight the abundant images and beauties of creation. — Leigh Hunt, On the Realities of Imagination

en: To cause to move faster; to quicken the motion of; to add to the speed of.
example: to accelerate the growth of a plant, the increase of wealth, etc.

en: To render fit, suitable, or correspondent; to adapt.
example: to accommodate ourselves to circumstances
exampleCn: 酒店可容纳三百名客人。

en: Agreement or concurrence of opinion, will, or action.
example: the accord of light and shade in painting

en: To heap up in a mass; to pile up; to collect or bring together (either literally or figuratively)
example: He wishes to accumulate a sum of money.
exampleCn: 经验是慢慢积累起来的。

en: To admit the knowledge of; to recognize as a fact or truth; to declare one's belief in
example: to acknowledge the being of a god

en: (followed by with) To furnish or give experimental knowledge of; to make (one) to know; to make familiar.
example: I think you should acquaint him with the realities of the situation.

en: A person who has the acute form of a disorder, such as schizophrenia.
example: The word ‘cafe’ often has an acute over the ‘e’.

en: Something that lies next to something else, especially the side of a right triangle that is neither the hypotenuse nor the opposite.
example: Because the conference room is filled, we will have our meeting in the adjacent room.

en: Characterized by aggression; unjustly attacking; prone to behave in a way that involves attacking or arguing.
example: an aggressive policy, war, person, nation
exampleCn: 公司采取了激进的扩张策略。

en: The state of being allied.
example: matrimonial alliances; an alliance between church and state, or between two countries

en: That which alternates with something else; vicissitude.
example: The flood and ebb tides alternate with each other.

en: Open to multiple interpretations.
example: The politician was criticized for his ambiguous statements and lack of precision.

en: (of a person or their character) Having or showing ambition; wanting a lot of power, honor, respect, superiority, or other distinction.
example: an ambitious person

en: A relationship of resemblance or equivalence between two situations, people, or objects, especially when used as a basis for explanation or extrapolation.
To act before (someone), especially to prevent an action.
example: The advocate plans to anticipate a part of her argument.

en: The entirety of means whereby a specific production is made existent or task accomplished.
Applause; applauding.
example: After the performance, the audience applauded for five minutes.

en: An implement, an instrument or apparatus designed (or at least used) as a means to a specific end (often specified), especially:
Anything arbitrary, such as an arithmetical value or a fee.
example: Benjamin Franklin's designation of "positive" and "negative" to different charges was arbitrary.

en: To move upward, to fly, to soar.
example: He ascended to heaven upon a cloud.

en: To attribute a cause or characteristic to someone or something.
example: One may ascribe these problems to the federal government; however, at this stage it is unclear what caused them.

en: A violent onset or attack with physical means, for example blows, weapons, etc.
example: to make assault upon a man, a house, or a town

en: An assertion; a section of source code which tests whether an expected condition is true.
example: He would often assert that there was life on other planets.

en: A characteristic or quality of a thing.
example: His finest attribute is his kindness.

en: (grammar) In some Indo-European languages, a prefix e- (a- in Sanskrit) indicating a past tense of a verb.
example: The money from renting out a spare room can augment a salary.

en: Of the same origin as claimed; genuine.
example: The experts confirmed it was an authentic signature.

en: The art or science of making and flying aircraft.
The state or level of consciousness where sense data can be confirmed by an observer.
example: I gradually passed from sleep to full awareness.

en: An area of low fertility and habitation, a desolate place.
example: The pine barrens are a site lonely enough to suit any hermit.

en: In a fundamental, essential or basic manner
example: They've added some fancy features, but it's basically still a car.

en: To endeavour to depress the price of, or prices in.
example: to bear a railroad stock

en: In comfortable circumstances as regards property; forehanded.
example: Will it be possible to have access to the room beforehand so that we can set up chairs?

en: The act of boycotting.
A gap or opening made by breaking or battering, as in a wall, fortification or levee / embankment; the space between the parts of a solid body rent by violence
example: A clear breach is when the waves roll over the vessel without breaking. A clean breach is when everything on deck is swept away.

en: A construction or natural feature that spans a divide.
example: With enough cable, we can bridge this gorge.

en: (often with "up") To make or become lively; to enliven; to animate.
example: We took a brisk walk yesterday.

en: Size, specifically, volume.
example: I understood the bulk of what you were saying, just one of two points I need to hear again.

en: A light blow or jolting collision.
example: the bump of veneration; the bump of acquisitiveness

en: An administrative unit of government; office.
example: a news bureau; a travel bureau; a service bureau; an employment bureau; the Citizens Advice Bureau

en: An act or instance of bursting.
example: The bursts of the bombs could be heard miles away.

en: Able and efficient; having the ability needed for a specific task; having the disposition to do something; permitting or being susceptible to something.
example: As everyone knew, he was capable of violence when roused.

en: A powdered substance produced by firing (calcining) calcium carbonate (limestone) and clay that develops strong cohesive properties when mixed with water. The main ingredient of concrete.
example: the cement of our love

en: Unified; sticking together; making up a whole.
To occupy exactly the same space.
example: The two squares coincide nicely.

en: The act of collapsing.
example: She suffered a terrible collapse after slipping on the wet floor.

en: To begin, start.
To do (something good) after (something bad) happens
example: It is hard work, but they will compensate you well for it.

en: Having sufficient skill, knowledge, ability, or qualifications.
example: He is a competent skier and an expert snowboarder.

en: An act of compiling code.
example: Samuel Johnson compiled one of the most influential dictionaries of the English language.

en: A protective substance that exists in the serum or other bodily fluid and is capable of killing microorganisms; complement.
example: The complement of blue is orange.

en: To yield assent; to accord; to acquiesce, agree, consent; to adapt oneself, to conform.
A comprehensive school.
example: When there are diametrically opposing views on a big issue that concerns millions of people, doing comprehensive research just makes sense.

en: To be made up of; to consist of (especially a comprehensive list of parts).
example: The parts are comprised by the whole.

en: Something that is compulsory or required.
example: The ten-dollar fee was compulsory.
exampleCn: 教育在某些国家是义务的。

en: To develop an idea; to form in the mind; to plan; to devise; to originate.
example: Assisted procreation can help those trying to conceive.

en: That which affects one’s welfare or happiness. A matter of interest to someone. The adposition before the matter of interest is usually over, about or for.
example: Judy's eyes filled with concern as she listened to the news report.
exampleCn: 环保是我们共同关切的事。

en: A solid mass formed by the coalescence of separate particles; a compound substance, a concretion.
example: The road was made of concrete that had been poured in large slabs.

en: To concentrate toward the essence by making more close, compact, or dense, thereby decreasing size or volume.
example: An abridged dictionary can be further condensed to pocket size.

en: To admit to the truth, particularly in the context of sins or crimes committed.
example: I confess to spray-painting all over that mural!

en: (chiefly in the plural) A boundary or limit.
To strengthen; to make firm or resolute.
To stand or meet facing, especially in competition, hostility or defiance; to come face to face with
example: We should confront him about the missing money.

en: Thorough, careful, or vigilant in one’s task performance.
example: He was a thoughtful and conscientious worker.

en: A person who favors maintenance of the status quo.
example: The curriculum committee at this university is extremely conservative.

en: (in the plural) Objects or facts that are coexistent, or in agreement with one another.
example: He is very consistent in his political choices: economy good or bad, he always votes Labour!

en: A person or object that is not normal.
To end a law, system, institution, custom or practice.
example: Slavery was abolished in the nineteenth century.

en: To be full to overflowing.
example: Wild animals abound wherever man does not stake his claim.

en: An absurdity.
(usually capitalized) The garden where Plato taught.
example: the military academy at West Point; a riding academy; the Academy of Music.; a music academy; a language academy

en: Having a secondary, supplementary or subordinate function by accompanying as a subordinate; aiding in a secondary way; being additional; being connected as an incident or subordinate to a principal; contributing or being contributory. Said of people and things, and, when of people, usually in a bad sense
example: She was accessory to the riot.

en: Agreement; harmony; conformity; compliance.
The state of being accurate; being free from mistakes, this exemption arising from carefulness; exactness; correctness
example: The value of testimony depends on its accuracy.

en: A sour substance.
example: acid fruits or liquors

en: The act of acknowledging
example: the acknowledgment of a favor

en: A state of being acquainted with a person; originally indicating friendship, intimacy, but now suggesting a slight knowledge less deep than that of friendship; acquaintanceship.
example: I know of the man; but have no acquaintance with him.

en: To encourage development or induce increased activity; to stimulate.
To stick fast or cleave, as a glutinous substance does; to become joined or united.
example: wax adhered to his finger

en: To be in contact or connection with.
example: The living room and dining room adjoin each other.

en: To administer
example: The job is to administrate the network.

en: A positive emotion including wonder and approbation; the regarding of another as being wonderful
example: They looked at the landscape in admiration.
exampleCn: 我对她的毅力充满敬意。

en: The act of adopting.
example: A Chinese baby girl was given away for adoption.

en: Being of advantage, beneficial.
Unfavorable; antagonistic in purpose or effect; hostile; actively opposing one's interests or wishes; contrary to one's welfare; acting against; working in an opposing direction.
example: adverse criticism

en: Someone whose job is to speak for someone's case in a court of law; a counsel.
example: Since she started working with her advocate, she has become much more confident.

en: A rod, wire, or other structure for receiving or transmitting radio, television signals etc.
example: The seabirds put on an astonishing aerial display.

en: Somebody who is wealthy.
example: They were affluent, but aspired to true wealth.

en: A temporally organized plan for matters to be attended to.
To make (an offence) worse or more severe; to increase in offensiveness or heinousness.
example: He aggravated the story.

en: To disturb or excite; to perturb or stir up (a person).
example: He was greatly agitated by the news.

en: A company that flies airplanes to transport people and goods.
A wing of a building, notably in a church separated from the nave proper by piers.
example: Do you want to seat window or aisle?

en: An alarm.
example: an airborne alert; ground alert

en: Any life form of extraterrestrial or extradimensional origin.
example: alien subjects, enemies, property, or shores

en: To state under oath, to plead.
example: The agency alleged my credit history had problems.

en: To make less severe, as a pain or difficulty.
example: Alcohol is often a cheap tool to alleviate the stress of a hard day.

en: An abridgement or summary of a longer publication.
example: He was wholly abstracted by other objects.

en: (usually a mass noun) Lodging in a dwelling or similar living quarters afforded to travellers in hotels or on cruise ships, or prisoners, etc.
The act of achieving or performing; a successful performance; accomplishment
example: Finishing the game does not give you a 100% score until you have unlocked all of the achievements.

en: The act of administering; government of public affairs; the service rendered, or duties assumed, in conducting affairs; the conducting of any office or employment; direction.
example: Successive US administrations have had similar Middle East policies.

en: A person who is in adolescence; someone who has reached puberty but is not yet an adult.
To promote or advantage.
The study of art or beauty.
example: Her most recent works have this quirky, half-serious 90's teen culture-inspired aesthetic.

en: A mass, assemblage, or sum of particulars; something consisting of elements but considered as a whole.
example: The aggregated soil.

en: To set aside for a purpose.
example: The bulk of K–12 education funds are allocated to school districts that in turn pay for the cost of operating schools.

en: To change the form or structure of.
Of or pertaining to analysis; resolving into elements or constituent parts
example: an analytical experiment

en: A fair valuation or estimate of merit, worth, weight, etc.; recognition of excellence.
example: We give to you this trophy as a token of our appreciation of all your years of service.
exampleCn: 他表达了对艺术的欣赏。

en: To make suitable; to suit.
example: Let no man appropriate the use of a common benefit.

en: To estimate.
example: NASA's Genesis spacecraft has on board an ion monitor to record the speed, density, temperature and approximate composition of the solar wind ions.

en: To determine, estimate or judge the value of; to evaluate
example: He assessed the situation.

en: The act of assuming, or taking to or upon oneself; the act of taking up or adopting.
example: His assumption of secretarial duties was timely.

en: Self-governing. Intelligent, sentient, self-aware, thinking, feeling, governing independently.
Inclination towards something; predisposition, partiality, prejudice, preference, predilection.
example: Our prejudices bias our views.

en: To quote; to repeat, as a passage from a book, or the words of another.
An established law.
Someone or something living at the same time, or of roughly the same age as another.
example: Cervantes was a contemporary of Shakespeare.

en: To give something that is or becomes part of a larger whole.
example: to contribute articles to a journal

en: Arousing controversy—a debate or discussion of opposing opinions.
